title: Tactics: Deflection and Decoys
summary: Learn to lure enemy pieces away from their critical squares.
description: Deflection (also called decoy) is a tactical pattern where you force an enemy piece to move to a less important square, allowing you to achieve your goal - often delivering checkmate or winning material. This is a key skill for GM-level tactics.
objectives: Identify critical squares that enemy pieces must defend
Learn to create threats that force pieces to move
Understand how deflection leads to checkmate or material gain
Master the concept of the 'sacrificial' deflection
plans: Find what your opponent must defend (king, key piece, critical square)
Create a threat that can only be answered by moving that defender
The deflection often involves a sacrifice
commonMistakes: Don't deflect if the piece can simply move and still defend
Calculate carefully - deflection must be forcing
Sometimes you need to attack something else first to set up deflection
completionSummary: You've learned to use deflection tactics to force favorable outcomes.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Recapture
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black moves the bishop to b6.

steps.title: Push e5
steps.instruction: Play e5! This attacks the knight on f6.
steps.coaching: The pawn on e5 attacks f6. The knight must move or be defended.

steps.title: Black defends
steps.instruction: Black pushes d5, attacking the bishop.
steps.coaching: The pawn attacks the bishop on c4, but more importantly, d5 blocks the e5 pawn from advancing.

steps.title: Capture on d5
steps.instruction: Capture on d5 with the bishop!

steps.title: Queen captures
steps.coaching: Now the queen is on d5, but look at Black's king position...

steps.title: The deflection
steps.instruction: Play Nc3! This attacks the queen on d5 AND creates a threat against the king.
steps.coaching: The queen must move. If she moves, we attack something else. If she captures the knight, we might have another tactic.
steps.hint: Move the knight to c3, attacking the queen!

steps.title: Queen moves
steps.coaching: The queen moves to a5, but we've gained a move and possibly queen access to the d-file.

steps.title: Take the d-file
steps.instruction: Move Rd1, taking control of the d-file.
